title: Tomato Rice
description: A South Indian–inspired tomato rice prepared with fragrant basmati rice, aromatic whole spices, and a rich tomato masala. This restaurant-style recipe delivers vibrant flavor with perfectly separate grains.

ingredients.title: For the Rice
items: 2 cups aged basmati rice (soaked 20 minutes, drained)

ingredients.title: For the Tomato Masala Base
items: 3 tbsp ghee (or sunflower oil for lighter flavor)
3 large ripe tomatoes, blanched, peeled & pureed

ingredients.title: For Finishing
items: 1 tbsp cashew nuts, lightly roasted in ghee
1 tbsp fresh coriander leaves, finely chopped
Few strands saffron, infused in 2 tbsp warm milk

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare the Rice
steps: Bring vegetable stock to a boil with turmeric and salt.
Add soaked basmati rice, cover, and cook on low until just done (fluffy, grains separate).
Spread on a tray, drizzle with a touch of ghee, and allow to cool slightly to prevent clumping.

instruction.title: Step 2: Tomato Masala Base
steps: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottom pan. Add cumin, fennel, cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon.
Once aromatic, add sliced onions. Sauté until golden brown (slow caramelization for depth).
Stir in ginger-garlic paste, cook until raw aroma disappears.
Add tomato puree and chopped tomatoes. Cook on medium until thick, rich, and oil begins to release.
Mix in red chili powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Adjust seasoning.

instruction.title: Step 3: Combine
steps: Gently fold in cooked rice with the tomato masala.
Add curry leaves, roasted cashews, and saffron milk.
Toss lightly so grains remain long and separate.

instruction.title: Step 4: Rest & Serve
steps: Cover with a lid and let it rest for 5 minutes off the heat.
Serve elegantly in a ring mold or copper handi, garnished with microgreens or edible flowers for a fine-dining touch.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Serve with onion raita or plain curd.
Garnish with roasted cashews and fresh coriander before serving.

chefTips: Tomatoes: Use fully ripe, slightly tart tomatoes for balance. Avoid overly watery ones.
Rice Texture: Cook rice separately, then fold into masala. This ensures non-sticky, aromatic grains.
Layered Flavor: Caramelizing onions slowly and cooking tomato until the oil separates are crucial for deep, rich flavor.
Presentation: Serve with a quenelle of raita, papad tuile, or micro coriander garnish for haute cuisine plating.
